posture: Quiet Monday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a-§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several recurring or previously-considered items were again reviewed and excluded as out-of-window: Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial and associated FOGO deferral (Waste Management Review, dated 12 November 2025) is prior-period background, not a fresh signal; Mackay Regional Council's three-part AI package (LUNA after-hours digital assistant from 1 July, development-planning task automation, agentic AI in corporate services) was published 24 June 2026 and is outside the 24-hour window; the NSW Early Adopter AI-in-planning grants (~$2.7M across 16 councils) and the AI Solutions Panel produced no new dated development today; and recycled-content road material, digital-twin and IoT-sensor items (Bathurst Regional Council spatial digital twin, national digital-twin launches) surfaced only via older or undated coverage. Overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 in Madrid, LGIU Top Thinkers 2026, Bloomberg Mayors Challenge winners) was either out-of-window or general network/policy content rather than a fresh, council-transferable signal. Today is a Monday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
